title: Cuba oil shortage — May 14 2026
narrative: Multiple outlets (BBC, others) — "Cuba Says It Has Run Out of Oil." The narrative: Cuba's state oil company Cupet declared a supply emergency. Official blame attributed to "US sanctions." Venezuela's Petroleos de Venezuela (PDVSA) shipments — Cuba's primary oil lifeline — reportedly delayed. Cuban government blaming external factors. US State Department likely to frame as sanctions working.
telemetry: Vessel tracking (AIS) for PDVSA tankers bound for Cuba in April-May 2026 — were ships actually en route or were they diverted/re-routed?
Cuban port authority records / shipping manifests — what did tankers actually discharge at Cuban ports vs. what was contracted?
Venezuelan domestic oil production and export data (PDVSA public reports, secondary sources) — did Venezuela actually have the oil to ship?
Russian oil shipments to Cuba (reported historically) — were there any arrivals in 2026, and did they stop?
Cuban energy grid data (if accessible via OSINT) — rolling blackouts, generation capacity vs. stated demand
US Treasury/OFAC records — any new Cuba-specific sanctions actions in April-May 2026 that could have blocked shipments?
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT] Supply emergency real; external blame narrative obscures systemic domestic factors. AIS and discharge data needed.
